MOUTH. ► Teeth, Objective. ► (A decidedly improved condition of the teeth; the carious teeth seem to secrete less, and the gums do not bleed so easily), (after second week), [1a] . ► The right eye-tooth, which was formerly rough, but became afterwards smooth again, is now rough, and commences to ache, particularly at the root of it; in the upper jaw, along the root of the teeth, pressure is very painful, particularly in the evening; it is mitigated temporarily by cold water (third and the following days), [13] . ► Subjective. ► In the morning the lower incisor teeth feel sharp, as if broken and the tongue feels painful on touching them for several days (fifth day), [1a] . [170.] ► Bluntness of the teeth, [16] . ► One drop of the one-sixteenth solution produced in the mouth a bluntness of the teeth, a sensation of sticking, contraction, and tension, after which the mucous membrane of the mouth became whitish and peeled off (after three day), [16] . ► Teeth feel warm , those of the upper jaw, especially on the left side; the incisor and canine are much warmer than any of the other; a few minutes afterwards there is considerable warmth in the pharynx; these sensations ceased in the course of an hour; the prover remarked particularly, that the heat or warmth was felt in the teeth, and not in the gums or alveolar processes, [6] . ► All the teeth of the left jaw seem to pain, especially those whose roots have been taken out, [9] . ► Toothache for a short time in the left lower jaw, [9] . ► Toothache on the right side, extending to the left thigh, the outer portion above the knee, [13] . ► The pain in the tooth on the right side disappears for a moment and jumps to the left thigh, on the outside above the knee (third day), [13] . ► The cold air in a room causes pain in the carious tooth of the right upper incisor, in the evening (after several hours), [1b] . ► Soon after taking the remedy transient pain in the right eye-tooth (in the evening), [13] . ► Digging pain in one of the lower incisors, towards the right (third day), [13] . [180.] ► Toothache; drawing in the left lower jaw (after four hours), [5] . ► (The right upper incisors are very sensitive on drinking cold water and also on inhaling cold air, particularly the one which is decayed; the same symptom some weeks before the proving), [1c] . ► Teeth so painful that he could not bite upon them, [16] . ► Gums. ► (A painful excoriation near the first lower molar tooth, on the right side), (after five weeks), [1a] . ► Tongue. ► Prickling of the tongue, and increase of saliva for some hours, and afterwards combined with smarting feeling of the palate as if something very acrid had been gargled; a feeling of tenderness and irritability in the larynx; coughing to clear the throat causes feeling of soreness, which shows an unusual degree of sensibility, [2] . ► General Mouth. ► *(A very painful little ulcer in the back part of the mouth, on the right side, in the angle of the upper and lower jaw; very troublesome during mastication and otherwise), [1] . ► Dryness of the left half of the palate and roof of the mouth, in the evening (fourth day), [3] . ► Greasy feeling in the mouth, immediately, [1a] . ► A solution of one-eighth caused in the mouth a violent pain as from hot water; the teeth became so painful that for two days he could not masticate, [16] . ► One drop of the one-forty-eighth solution produced in the mouth contractions and pricklings, and a disagreeable sour taste, [16] . ► Saliva. [190.] ► Salivation after five minutes, so that he is frequently obliged to spit, lasting ten minutes, followed by headache, with which the salivation decreases and after an hour disappears, [5] . ► Five minutes after taking, there commenced an increase of the flow of saliva, which caused him to spit constantly for about ten minutes, when he began to feel a pain in the head, a sensation as if the head was too heavy and would drop down from one side to the other, a pressing outward from within; the flow of saliva after this pain had established itself began to diminish, and in about an hour ceased entirely, [6] . ► Soon after taking the medicine there commenced the same salivation, with a dull heaviness and pain in the whole head, [6] . ► Much tenacious saliva, on waking at night, previous to the diarrha, [15] . ► He wakes up after midnight with a large quantity of viscid, tasteless saliva in the mouth, burning-pinching pain in the stomach, and with a sensation of distension from flatulency; after the passage of some wind the pain is increased, and he cannot pass any more; after a copious pappy evacuation of the bowels the pains seem to concentrate themselves in the region of the navel; after the evacuation the pain is diminished; on returning to his room; however, it returns again, and he has a second passage, accompanied with pain in the region of the navel; a third passage at 7 A.M. (third day); the same diarrha, at 4 A.M. (sixth day); a copious pappy evacuation, after rising (eighth day); several pappy evacuations (tenth day); two watery stools, immediately after rising in the morning (twelfth day); pappy loose passage, in the morning after rising (thirteenth and fourteenth days); none (fifteenth day); two (sixteenth day); one again (seventeenth day), [15] . ► (The mouth is not so full of mucus in the morning as usual; it is less around the teeth; in rinsing it there is less blood and viscid mucus than usual), (after several days), [1c] . ► Taste. ► Sweetish taste in the throat at night (fourth day), [3] . ► Acid taste, and greasy feeling in the mouth, immediately, [2] . ► The taste is quite intolerable, [17] . ► Acrid offensive taste comes from the roots of the teeth, [3] . [200.] ► Acrid putrid taste from the root of the right upper incisor, on which there is fixed an artificial tooth; (the next morning), [3] . ► Taste in the mouth of what he has eaten; worse in the afternoon, [11] . ► A saltish taste in the mouth, in the morning on awakening, until breakfast (second day), [15] . ► Taste like ink; seems to proceed from a lower tooth on the left side; in the evening (second day), [1b] . THROAT. ► Objective. ► Hawking of bloody mucus from low down, [1] . ► Subjective. ► Feeling of warmth in the throat, [6] . ► Constriction in the throat, with difficult deglutition, at first in the forenoon (after three hours); it begins towards evening (next day), [9] . ► Constriction in the throat; pressure and sensation of fulness in the region of the stomach; frequent stale, disgusting eructations, with inclination to vomit; from 1 drachm of 1/48th solution (about 1 1/4 gr.) within one hour and a half, all symptoms disappeared, [16] . ► Constriction of the throat, rumbling in the abdomen, pressure in the stomach and burning, eructations, retching, for two hours, followed by constipation for two days (after 10 drops of the 1/16th solution, about 5/8 gr.), [16] . ► Constriction in the left side of the throat, with pressure in the right arm, [9] . ► Sore throat, with difficult deglutition; his throat, as far as below the larynx, felt so sore that the bread, although masticated very thoroughly, could not be swallowed without the greatest pain; after smelling the acid, 5 P.M., until the next morning after breakfast; the same morning, hawking up of much phlegm, mixed with some blood; during the day abatement of the symptoms, [1] . [210.] ► A singular sensation as though the passage from the mouth to the nose was wide open, during his walk in the open air, [1c] . ► Fauces, Pharynx, and sophagus. ► Sensation of heat in the fauces the next morning, [13] . ► Soreness in the pharynx, extending down below the larynx; swallowing bread is painful, [1] . ► In the back of the throat, low down, at the entrance of the sophagus, more to the left side, a sensation as if it were somewhat raw, or a though an ulcer would form; it was indefinite and slightly painful, yet it seemed very troublesome, and twice, while hawking, he thought that blood would come from it, in the morning and forenoon (eleventh and twelfth days), [1a] . ► Swallowing. ► The greatest difficulty was experienced in getting him to swallow anything, [19] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Appetite increased, [8] . ► Better appetite than usual (part of S. 307), [1a] . ► Extraordinary appetite after diarrha, [1] . ► Voracious appetite, [8] . ► Excessive hunger, [8] . [220.] ► He immediately began to long for coffee, for which he had lost his appetite, on commencing the proving, [1a] . ► With his usual good appetite, he nevertheless is soon satisfied (third, fourth, and following days), [1c] . ► Very soon satisfied; therewith the food tastes good and is well digested (first week), [1a] . ► Very soon satisfied the first week; the second week, he began to eat more in the evening, and the increase of appetite in the evening lasted for several weeks, [1] . ► The same speedy satiety , without any gastric disturbance, in winter, spring, and summer, during different provings, [1] . ► He is immediately satisfied after plate of soup, without the slightest sensation of nausea (fourth day), [1a] . ► He eats very little, though his appetite is very good (second and following days), [1a] . ► The appetite is diminished, he wants something "piquant," [11] . ► Aversion to coffee (nineteenth day), [1a] . ► He relishes wine no more; the desire for it is decreased for several weeks, [1a] . ► Thirst. [230.] ► *More thirst (part of S. 333), [1a] . ► Thirst at night, [3] . ► Eructation. ► Eructations (part of S. 257), [16] . ► Eructations and slight nausea in the evening, [20] . ► Eructations and retching, [16] . ► Eructations and discharge of flatulency, frequently , [8] . ► Eructations of wind and sickness of the stomach, very soon, [5] . ► Frequent stale, disgusting eructations (parts of S. 206), [16] . ► Frequently flat, nauseous eructations, with inclination to vomit, [16] . ► Sour eructations, [16] . [240.] ► Twenty drops of the 1/48th solution, taken in water, excited frequent sour eructations; sensation of heat and a disagreeable flat taste in the mouth, [16] . ► Frequent acid eructations, with pyrosis and passage of flatulency, in the afternoon (second day), [5] . ► Sour, bitter eructations after dinner (in five hours), [5] . ► Burning eructations (part of S. 207), [16] . ► Heartburn. ► Pyrosis, with nausea (in two hours and a half), [5] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► A frequent sensation as though nausea or a kind of vertigo would set in, but which does not (after ten minutes), [1a] . ► Nausea (part of S. 103), [5] ; (part of S. 257), [16] . ► Nausea, without inclination to vomit, with dulness of the head, [1] . ► Nausea and heaviness above the eyes, [5] . ► Some sickness of the stomach (after four hours), [5] . [250.] ► Sickness of stomach, continuing from three to four hours, after taking the acid, [4] . ► Sickness of the stomach, with some desire to vomit, soon after second dose, [6] . ► Continual sickness of the stomach, with vertigo and headache (after four hours), [5] . ► Constant great sickness of the stomach, with general heat (after two hours), [5] . ► Inclination vomit (part of S. 206), [16] . ► Retching (part of S. 207), [16] . ► Violent and incessant retching and vomiting (immediately), [19] . ► He vomits several times with difficulty a clear, viscid fluid, with coagulated white pieces, having no connection with the burning in the mouth, nor the symptoms accompanying it; the whole day, he felt nausea, eructations, and lassitude (after 30 drops of 1/16th solution, about 2 grains), [16] . ► Rumbling in the stomach, [15] . ► Rumbling in the stomach and urging, as in diarrha, after eating (thirteenth and fourteenth days), [13] . [260.] ► Decidedly uncomfortable sensation in the stomach in the forenoon (after second dose), [3] . ► Heat in the stomach, before his meal, which disappears after it; then heaviness in the stomach, and after some hours again heat; worse during exercise, [11] . ► Burning in the stomach, [16] . ► Burning-pinching pain in the stomach before diarrha, [1] . ► Pressure in the stomach, with burning, [16] . ► Pressure in the stomach (about 5/8 grain), (part of S. 207), [16] . ► Pressure and sensation of fulness in the region of the stomach (part of S. 206), [16] . ► Sensation, between meals, as of pressure from a weight in the stomach, simulating indigestion (after third day), [8] . ► Sensitiveness of the region of the stomach to pressure, [11] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Pinching in the region of the spleen at 11 A.M. (fourth and following days), [15] . [270.] ► (A pain which he has had for six days in the region in the spleen, reaching to the hips, disappears immediately after one drops 30th, and does not return), [10] . ► Umbilical. ► Sensation of faintness, like an emptiness, in the region of the navel, with a desire to draw a deep breath; it is relieved by bandaging; in the forenoon before he has eaten anything; it gets better after eating; in the evening, it is more like a slight burning; when occupied, he does not observe it, [11] . ► During diarrha, pain more particularly in the region of the navel (after several hours), [1b] . ► Pinching in region of navel, inclination to diarrha, and a copious watery evacuation, at 2 A.M. (seventh day), [15] . ► General Abdomen. ► Rumbling in the abdomen, [11] ; (part of S. 207), [16] . ► Rumbling in bowels, with erratic pain, in the forenoon, [6] . ► Much rumbling from flatulency (ninth day), [15] . ► Frequent passage of flatus and eructations; it leaves him immediately more comfortable, and with a feeling as if it was the last, but it is not so, for in a few minutes everything is renewed in the same order, succession, and feeling as before; and so on for two or three days, with the only difference of being at longer intervals than it was at first , [8] . ► Passes much inodorous wind, [11] . ► He passes more inodorous flatus, with much noise (after several days), [1b] , [51] . [280.] ► Offensive flatus, in the morning, [15] . ► Small, very offensive wind precedes the evacuation (second and eighth day), [1a] . ► Small, excessively offensive discharges of wind, smelling like a mixture of carburetted, sulphuretted, and phosphuretted hydrogen; the odor was exceedingly persistent in the forenoon, several hours before his meal, and in the afternoon, two hours after eating (second day), [1a] . ► Inclination to draw up the muscles of the abdomen, with great appetite, [11] . ► Inclination to draw up the abdominal muscles, with great appetite, [15] . ► Sensation in the left side of abdomen as if a pain were about to arise and wind to be discharged, without either taking place (half an hour after fifth dose), [10] . ► (After eating herring, a very unpleasant sensation in the abdomen, and a very discontented mood), (after several hours), [1b] . ► (Immediately after partaking of some pieces of watermelon, some pain in the abdomen, which is not generally to case), (after one hour), [1a] . ► Pain in the left side of the abdomen, in the region of the spleen (after one hour), [10] . ► A sensation of warmth in the abdomen, with a pressure towards the bladder, in the night (first day), [1a] . [290.] ► Feeling of accumulated flatulence, [15] . ► A pressing pain in the abdomen on the left side, and also in the left arm, in the evening, [9] . ► Suddenly and acute pain in the left side of the abdomen, above the hip hindering respiration (after two hours), [10] . ► Shooting pain in bowels, extending toward the right side, as if from wind, sometimes very acute (evening), [6] . ► Slight colic, immediately after eating fish with vinegar, [1c] . ► Jerkings in left side of abdomen (after two hours), [10] . ► The pain is increased after passage of flatulence, before the diarrha, [15] . ► Continual dull pain in the inguinal region on both side, and deeply situated, [8] . ► Burning for a short time in the right inguinal region, in the afternoon, [13] . ► Burning from the left side of the breast down to the groin, [11] . [300.] ► Pain from the left side of the chest to the groins, increased by deep respiration, particularly in the groin and back, like a stitch, from 9 to 10 A.M., [11] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Determination of blood to the anus after drinking wine, [1a] . ► (The protrusion of the anus during evacuation, habitual to him, was very quickly, and to a considerable degree, diminished for several weeks), (only returned on resuming coffee and wine), [1a] . ► (A more comfortable feeling in the anus than is usual with him; in one subject to piles), (second day), [1a] . ► Constriction of the anus in attempting to emit flatulence, [1c] . ► He feels as if wind was retained in the anus, [1b] . ► Within and around the anus, violent itching (fifteenth and sixteenth days); continues around the anus until the fifth week, [1a] . ► Indefinite desire, small odorless wind, and pappy evacuation at 10 A.M. (fourth day); after small, excessively fetid discharges of flatus, a sudden urging, then a thin, pappy passage without any odor, and at the same time a very copious discharge of urine, before dinner; he had eaten peaches before, which, however, never acted that way; and after the passage, a better appetite than usual (eighth day); he observed the tertian type the second, fourth (sixth), and eighth days; the evacuation is more regular than usual (fifth week), [1a] . ► Ineffectual desire for a passage in the evening (eighth day), [15] . ► Hasty pressure for a passage, as in diarrha, and a soft evacuation, at 2 P.M. (five hours after fourth dose); was formerly very regular in his bowels; passage protracted and very small (next day) on omitting the remedy, [10] . STOOL. ► Diarrha. [310.] ► Inclination to diarrha, two passages (first day), [5] . ► (The soft, small passage, which he has every morning after drinking coffee, and again late in the evening, with ineffectual urging and protrusion of hæmorrhoids, changes after four to five days, into copious natural evacuations), [1c] . ► Free evacuation of the bowels twice a day, [3] . ► Two hard passage (second day), [5] . ► His passage is more loose than common, [11] . ► A large, pappy, yellowish-brown passage, of a very strong disagreeable smell of fæces, together with tenesmus, to which the prover is liable, although less protrusion of the anus than formerly (second day), [1a] . ► Constipation. ► *Constipated (second day), (part of S. 207), [16] . ► Constipated for two days (after 10 drops 1/16th), [16] . ► Stool delayed and very scanty (after omitting the remedy), [10] . ► (The passage (from the bowels) seem to occur every other day, and every day at a later hour), [1] . ► The evacuation (from the bowels) becomes protracted, insufficient, and lumpy, [1b] , [51] . [320.] ► (During from a stool, some nodules were at last pressed out with some difficulty), (seventeenth day), [1b] . ► No passage (third day), [1a] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► A sensation somewhat resembling numbness or burning in the bladder (part of S. 550), [1] . ► After urination, a pain as if it were above the neck of the bladder in the afternoon, [1b] . ► Before and after urination, a pain in the lower part of the bladder; there is also pain on pressure (first and second days), [1b] . ► Dull pains in the region of the bladder (first week), [1a] . ► Dragging towards the bladder, with feeling of warmth in the abdomen, [8] . ► Pressure on the bladder, with sensation of warmth in the abdomen (first day), [1a] . ► Urethra. ► *An intolerable burning in the urethra, during and after urination , for five minutes, in the morning (ninth day), (in one who never had gonorrha), [15] . [330.] ► Frequent desire to pass water after several days, [1c] . ► Micturition. ► Free discharge of light-colored urine, very frequent, of sufficiently large, but not increased quantity, leaving him more comfortable ; during and after the discharge he feels a kind of elasticity (in the muscles?), of the urinary organs, with a subsequent agreeable sensation; he drinks less than usual, [8] . ► Has to get up twice at night to void his urine, which is quite unusual with him; the night is cooler than the previous one (fifth day), [1a] . ► More urination, and afterwards more thirst (fifth day), [1a] . ► Urine increased, and of a clear color, after several days, [1b] . ► Copious evacuation of urine, with the diarrha, [1] . ► Very copious discharge of urine (part of S. 307), [1a] . ► Less urination the next morning, [1a] . ► Decidedly less voiding of urine, but the urine is not of a darker color (second and third days), [1a] . ► The urine has diminished in quantity, and has a decided fragrancy (like Benzoic acid), in the evening and morning (second day); disappeared (fifth day), [1c] . ► Urine. [340.] ► Pungent and strong odor of the urine, which was freely discharged, in the evening (fourth day), [3] . ► A very acrid and strong odor of the urine (fifteenth and sixteenth days), [1a] . ► His urine frequently smells very offensive, as far as the sixth week, [1a] . ► His habitual whitish sediment in the urine is also mixed with a very copious purple-colored one (third day); its purple-colored sediment disappeared fifth day, [1c] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main

Itching in the larynx, which causes him to hawk and to swallow. Short frequent cough, mostly dry, white, frothy sputa. Itching under the ribs (l.). Difficult respiration (afternoon and evening). During respiration wheezing (hydrothorax). Pain in r. shoulder-joint. Pain in r. shoulder-joint, extending toward fingers, with sensation as if air were passing down. Trembling in the biceps and triceps of r. arm. Slight lameness in r. arm (has some difficulty in writing). Rheumatic pains in l. arm from shoulder to elbow, with lameness. The l. forearm and hand asleep (in the morning). Numbness and lameness in l. forearm and hand (morning). Pain in l. index finger; the whole finger is painful internally. Weakness and numbness of the hands and head. Constant redness of the hands, esp. the palms of the hands. Acute prickings, as with needles, in the fingers. Thumbs and fingers inflamed, with acute throbbing pains. Sensation of a splinter under nail. The nails grow more rapidly. Brittleness of the nails. Panaritium; also simple onychia. Œdematous swelling up to the abdomen. Varicose veins. Acute stitches in r. hip-bone. Lameness in l. hip. Pain in r. knee-joint. The l. leg falls easily asleep. Burning stitches under the soles of the feet (in the morning). Feet hot and burn. Soreness between the toes. Soreness of all his corns. Lassitude. Loss of strength. Limbs go to sleep, although he does not lie on them. Increased ability to exercise his muscles without fatigue, regardless of the most excessive heat in summer or cold in winter. Violent jerking, burning pains, confined to a small spot.

Increased ability to exercise his muscles without fatigue ; is less affected by excessive heat in summer or cold in winter. Sensation of going to sleep of side not lain on. Limbs go to sleep, although he does not lie on them. Lassitude. Great diurnal languor. θ Secondary syphilis. Loss of strength ; great prostration and fear of death. Coccyodynia, with excessive aching, especially in rheumatic subjects. Progressive locomotor ataxia. Tabes dorsalis with great excitement of sexual system. Frequent urging to make water. Twice a day : evacuations. Twenty-four hours : six to eight evacuations. Every day for a week at same hour : discharge of yellow bilious matter. Every morning : headache. Every evening : heat returns lasting three to four hours. Alternate days : aggravation an hour later each time. Every three or four days : scar begins to itch. Right : pain in temples ; numbness of bones around ear ; itching on side on nose ; lower jaw swollen ; violent pains at root of eye-tooth ; pressure on gum over eye-tooth ; back part of mouth, painful little ulcer ; hypochondrium sensitive to pressure ; itching on side of nose ; pain in shoulder-joint ; slight lameness of arm ; trembling of biceps and triceps of arm. Left : dulness in side of head ; pain in temple ; side of head retarded in growth ; eye seems smaller ; pain in inner canthus ; lachrymal fistula on side ; pimple on wing of nose ; pressing pain in arm ; itching under ribs ; lobe of liver enlarged and indurated ; pain from side of chest to groin ; rumbling pain most acute in side ; itching on breast ; itching of nipple ; rheumatic pains in arm ; pain in index-finger ; first and second phalanx of index-finger swollen ; one-sided sweat. Right to left : pain in temples. Symptoms seem to go from below up. Within outward : pressing in temples. As if danger menaced him ; brain feels as if on the verge of being struck with apoplexy ; as if in an earthquake ; as of a cold wind blowing under lids even in a warm room ; as if sand were in eyes ; as if he must wink ; as if something were in eye that could be rubbed out ; as if air was passing down from shoulder-joint to fingers ; pain as if in bone of left index-finger ; as if a burning vapor was emitted from pores of whole body. Pain : in right temple ; in sutures behind ears ; from nape extending through centre of head to forehead ; in left inner canthus ; in chronic nasitis ; in tongue ; from left side of chest to groin ; about navel ; in abdomen ; urine painful in passing ; in third cervical vertebra ; in os coccygis ; in right shoulder-joint ; in right knee-joint ; in all the limbs ; in bones. Violent pains at root of right eye-tooth. Severe pain : in lower abdomen. Stitches : in right hip-bone ; in soles. Sharp darting pains about half an inch in length, like streaks of lightning. Sticking pains at root of thumb-nail. Pinching in region of spleen ; in stomach ; about navel. Griping in abdomen. Burning pain : on small spots of skin. Bruised pain : in sacrum. Pressing pain : in region of spleen ; in left arm ; in vertex. Compressing pain : in temples. Rumbling pain : in left side. Rheumatism : in arms and shoulders. Dull heavy headache : in forehead. Sore pain : at heart. Aching : in bladder ; in os sacrum ; in lumbar region. Burning : in eyes ; in ears ; in stomach ; about navel ; before and after urination ; in hands. Soreness : of larynx ; at heart ; between toes ; of all the corns. Raw feeling : of trachea. Irritability : of throat ; of larynx. Sensitiveness : of teeth. Constriction : in throat ; of anus. Oppression : of chest. Heat : at stomach. Distension : of abdomen. Fulness : in head ; in epigastrium ; in both spermatic cords ; of glans penis ; lower part of scrotum. Pricking : of tongue ; in fingers. Tingling : of tongue. Emptiness : in region of navel. Singing : in ears. Ringing : in ears. Roughness : of teeth. Dryness : of larynx ; of trachea. Dulness : of head ; in occiput. Pressing : in both temples ; in occiput ; as if behind right eyeball ; in epigastrium. Heaviness : above eyes ; of teeth. Weight : in stomach. Lameness : in back ; in hip ; in legs ; in arms ; in shoulders ; in left hip. Elastic feeling : in urinary organs. Sinking weakness : in head. Numbness : in forehead ; in hands ; in bones around ear ; of tongue ; of hands. Sensation of going to sleep of side lain on ; legs go to sleep easily. Itching : of head ; in canthi ; of fistula on left side of cheek ; in both ears ; on right side of nose ; under ribs ; within and around anus ; on left breast ; on right side of nose ; of right nipple ; in larynx ; of skin ; of ulcers. Atrophy of brain. Chronic irritation of mucous membranes. Emaciation. θ Dropsy. Muscles soft and flabby. θ Chronic diarrhœa. Alopecia. Goitre. Liver enlarged and indurated. Dropsy of drunkards. Acute suppurations. Threatening gangrene in swollen scrotum and penis. Varicose veins of legs. Nevus, flat. Varicose veins, obstinate and long standing cases, especially in women who have borne many children. Varicose ulcers. Pains in the bones. Swelling of bones. Osteitis. Exostoses and nightly pains in bones. Diseases of bones, particularly of long bones ; suppurate and get better and worse periodically ; pains < at night, with great prostration. Diseases of bones ; particularly of long bones ; caries and necrosis, especially when they are of a psoric or syphilitic nature ; or from abuse of mercury. Promotes expulsion of necrotic bones. Syphilis. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Fl. ac.]. Touch : sore pain in os coccygis. Pressure : scab on cheek painful to ; right hypochondrium sensitive ; liver sensitive ; of clothes > ; emptiness in region of navel ; > aching in os sacrum and lumbar region ; makes pain extend from os coccygis through to bladder. Bedsores on parts which do not sweat much. Decubitus. θ Typhus. Complaints of old age, also premature old age, in consequence of syphilitic-mercurial dyscrasia. Young people look very old. Weakly constitutions, sallow skin, emaciation. Secondary syphilis ; syphilis infantum. Boy, æt. 9, after scarlet fever ; caries of temporal bone. Man, æt. 24, primary sore two years ago which disappeared under use of blue pill, and topical application of black wash, four months afterwards had secondary ulceration of throat, which also temporarily healed under blue pill ; syphilis. Mrs. G., suffering for a month ; diarrhœa. Man, æt. 30, secondary symptoms for five or six months ; syphilis. Man, æt. 32, formerly much troubled with scrofulous eruptions ; ulceration of uvula. Man, æt. 40, subject to indigestion and attacks of bilious vomiting and diarrhœa nearly all his like ; diarrhœa and vomiting. Mr. B., æt. 50 ; aural itching. Man, æt. 59, weak constitution, hard drinker, has enlargement of liver ; dropsy.

mental_emotional.text: Indifference towards those loved best; inability to realize responsibility; buoyancy. Mentally elated and gay

Uncommon buoyancy of mind; fears nothing and is self-satisfied. Disposition to be exceedingly anxious, causing perspiration. Aversion to his own family. Sensation as if danger menaced him. Forgetfulness of dates and of his common employment.

Great loss of memory ; forgets almost everything. Forgetfulness every evening ; good memory in the morning. Forgetfulness : in his daily employment ; of dates ; on making notes he mistakes "right" for "left". Mental weakness. θ Ascites. Mental excitability. Feeling of indifference towards those he loves best, has no objection to their presence but does not care to converse with them, yet it strangers or mere acquaintances come in will enter into animated conversation. Aversion to his own family, bordering on insanity. Gay disposition, everything is satisfactory. Excessive hilarity ; great buoyancy of mind, happy, serene. Greatly depressed in mind. θ Ascites. Disposition to be exceedingly anxious, causing sweat ; > in morning than in evening. Sensation as if danger menaced him, but without fear. Felt certain that something dreadful would happen, with dulness in head, mostly in front, left side, extending into middle of brain. Fear of apoplexy. Anxiety. θ Ascites. Irritable, disagreeable mood. Moodiness in evening, > in morning. Very ill humored. θ Ascites. Discontent and excessive ill humor followed by indifference and forgetfulness, and finally by perfect contentment and uncommonly gay disposition of mind. Congestion of blood mostly to forehead, with dulness in occiput toward night. Feeling in brain as if on the verge of being struck with apoplexy. Vertigo with sickness of stomach. A kind of sinking weakness, has to sit down. Feeling as if in an earthquake. Sensation of weakness, like numbness in head, same in hands.

head.text: Alopecia. Caries of skin. Pressure on sides of head from within outward. Caries of ossicles and mastoid, with copious discharge; worse warmth (Silica; worse cold). Exostosis

Vertigo with sickness of the stomach. Congestion of blood to the head (forehead). Dulness (towards night) in the occiput. Dulness and pressure in occiput. Sensation of numbness in the forehead. Heaviness above the eyes, with nausea, on motion. Compressing pain in the temples. Headache > by profuse flow of urine. Severe pressing pain in temples, from within outward. Pain along the sutures. Caries of the temporal bones. Sensation of weakness, like numbness in the head (and hands). Itching of the head. Falling off of the hair; the new hair is dry and breaks off. Baldness.

Sensation of numbness in forehead. Congestion of blood to forehead with dulness in occiput towards night. Heaviness above eyes, with nausea, < by motion. Severe pressing in both temples, from within outward. Compressing pain in temples. Slight pain in right temple, five hours after in left. Headache in sutures behind ears at 3 P. M. Headache accompanied by congestion of blood to head, sensation of numbness, with heaviness and compressive pains. Headache every morning > by micturition. Sensation of weakness, like numbness in head. Numbness in head and hands. Dull, heavy headache. θ Dyspepsia. Congestive headache. Dulness and pressure in occiput. Dullness of occiput ; towards right in occiput. Pressure on both sides of occiput. Headache in occiput, with fulness in head. Headache from nape, extending through centre of head to forehead ; also dull feeling in head. Atrophy of brain. Sensation of numbness in forehead. Scalp sensitive. Pain along the sutures. Itching of head ; baldness. Congestion of blood to scalp ; hair becomes dry, breaks off and falls out. Falling off of hair. Hair falling out after typhoid fever. Alopecia areata. Must comb hair frequently, it mats so at the end. θ Pica polonica. Scaly eruptions on head. Crusta lactea, dry, scaly, itches very much, makes bald. Caries of temporal bone, discharging offensive-smelling pus periodically ; whole left side of head retarded in growth, left eye seems smaller. Syphilitic caries of bones of skull.

eyes.text: Sensation as of wind blowing through eyes. Lachrymal fistula. Violent itching of inner canthus
Violent itching in the canthi. Burning in the eyes. Pressure, as if it were behind r. eyeball. Fistula lachrymalis. Sensation of sand in the eyes, or as if a fresh wind was blowing on them.

Clearness of sight and increased power of vision. Pleasant sensation as though the eyelids were opened wider, or eyes more prominent, whereby circle of vision becomes enlarged, the sight clearer and he feels a luxurious enjoyment in looking at the same things he is accustomed to see every day. Retinal excitement with red photopsia. Flashes like lightning before the eyes. Amaurosis ; dreaming of fire. Dark spot before eye, < from reading. Heaviness above eyes, with nausea ; < on motion. Pressure, as if behind right ball. Burning in the eyes. Pain in left inner canthus. Sensation as of a cold wind blowing under lids, even in a warm room, must tie them up and keep them warm. Sensation of sand in eyes, or as if a fresh wind was blowing on them. Chronic ophthalmia ; feeling of sand in eye, must wink constantly. Feeling as if he must wink, as if something was in eye that could be rubbed out. Increased lachrymation. Itching in canthi. Fistula lachrymalis. Lachrymal fistula on left side, of one year's duration ; a clear yellow scab on cheek near inner angle, which is but slightly red and painful to pressure ; every three or four days it begins to itch and grow moist, then heals again ; sometimes painful before it opens.

nose.text: Chronic nasal catarrh with ulceration of the septum; nose obstructed and dull heavy pain in forehead
Red, swollen, inflamed nose. Obstruction of the nose. Fluent coryza.

Fluent coryza. Cold water produces coryza. Posterior nares feel expanded, during a walk. Chronic nasitis, with pain ; chronic obstruction of nose, with dull, heavy pain in forehead, followed by semi-fluent coryza. Chronic nasal catarrh, with ulceration of septum. Pimple on left wing of nose perforated and left an opening as large as a pea. Pimple with extensive inflamed base on top of nose. Red, swollen, inflamed nose. θ Rhinitis. Itching on right side of nose.

mouth_throat.text: Dental fistula, with persistent bloody, salty discharge. Syphilitic ulceration of throat, which is very sensitive to cold. Teeth feel warm. Affects teeth and bones of upper jaw

The teeth feel warm (l. upper jaw). Fistula (near the r. eye-tooth) with great sensitiveness of the upper jaw to the touch. Sensation of roughness (lower incisor teeth). Toothache from cold drink; or > until water becomes warm in mouth. Rapid caries of teeth. Acrid, foul taste from the roots of the teeth. Increased flow of saliva. Increased flow of saliva (with sneezing; with pricking of the tongue.). In the morning the mouth and teeth are full of mucus. The posterior nares feel expanded during a walk. Tongue deeply and widely fissured in all directions, with a large, deep phagedenic-looking ulcer in the centre. Throat peculiarly sensitive to cold, slightest exposure resulting in inflammation, with increase of pain and impeded deglutition. Constriction in the throat with difficult deglutition; in the morning hawking up of much phlegm which is mixed with blood.

The teeth feel warm (felt side, upper jaw). Heaviness of the teeth. Sensation of roughness (lower incisor teeth). Great sensitiveness of teeth ; cannot be plugged. Toothache < from cold drink ; or > until water becomes warm in mouth. Mouth and teeth coated with mucus in morning. Violent pains at root of right eye-tooth, with frequent discharge of pus. Fistula at root of tooth, or of gum ; teeth exceedingly sensitive. Acrid, foul taste from roots of teeth. Rapid caries of teeth. The teeth come late in children ; wisdom teeth come late. Great sensitiveness to pressure on gum over right eye-tooth. θ Fistula dentalis. Acrid foul taste from roots of teeth. Tongue always more or less tender, feeling of rigidity in it, with restricted mobility. θ Secondary syphilis. Tongue painful when talking. Tingling and numbness of tongue. Tongue vivid red at tip and edges, coated yellow in centre. Tongue whitish and dry. θ Ascites. Tongue deeply and widely fissured in all directions, with a large, deep, phagedenic looking ulcer in centre. Ulcer on and under tongue. Increased flow of saliva ; with sneezing ; with pricking of tongue. Mouth and teeth are full of mucus, in morning. Teeth and mouth covered with dark mucus. θ Typhus. Viscid, tasteless saliva in mouth ; before stool ; at night on waking. A very painful little ulcer in back part of mouth, on right side, in angle of upper and lower jaw ; very troublesome during mastication and otherwise. Constriction in throat with difficult deglutition ; in the morning, hawking of phlegm mixed with blood. Red blotches, size of half pea, over palate and sides of cheeks, bleeding easily. Tonsils, uvula and soft palate of a livid red color, and greatly swollen. Excessive suffering on swallowing or talking. Sleep disturbed by accumulation of mucus in fauces. Throat irritable and peculiarly sensitive to cold, slightest exposure resulting in inflammatory action with increase of pain and impeded deglutition. Syphilitic affections of fauces and tongue. Soft palate and uvula intensely red and much tumefied ; breath fetid, voice nasal, articulation indistinct and unintelligible. θ Secondary syphilis. Ulceration of uvula a portion of which seems only attached by a thread ; ulceration of nose ; fetid smell from nose and mouth ; hardness of hearing ; stubborn exanthema. Chronic catarrhal inflammation of pharynx and fauces, with ptyalism : especially if syphilitic.

stomach_abdomen.text: Heaviness and weight in stomach heat in stomach before meals. Sour eructations. Averse to coffee, wants fancy dishes. Stomach symptoms relieved by tight clothes. Desire for highly seasoned food. Craves cold water, hungry. Warm drinks produce diarrhœa
Soreness over liver. Flatus and eructations

Hunger predominates. Thirst, craves refreshing drinks. Aversion to coffee. Eructation and discharge of flatulency. Frequent eructations. Nausea, eructations, and lassitude. Sickness of the stomach, with general heat. Fulness and pressure in epigastrium. Pressure from weight in the stomach, between meals. Heat in the stomach before the meal. Bilious vomiting after slight errors in diet, with increased alvine discharges, preceded by tormina. Frequent passages of flatus and belchings, which relieve. Great tension and dropsical swelling of the abdomen. Pinching in the region of the spleen (extending to the hips), 11 a.m. Pressing pain in the region of the spleen and l. arm. Sensation of emptiness in the region of the navel, with desire to draw a deep breath; > by bandaging and eating.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Fl. ac.]. Hunger predominates ; great appetite in evening. Loss of appetite. θ Dropsy. Speedy satiety. Thirst ; craves refreshing drinks. Craves cold water, and is continually hungry. Desire : for highly seasoned and piquant things ; for wine. Aversion to coffee. Complaints worse from sweet things and from coffee. After eating salmon. θ Bilious diarrhœa. During the day, mainly soon after drinking, especially warm drinks, such as tea, cocoa, beef tea, bilious diarrhœa. Sweat lessened after meals. Nausea and eructations, with lassitude. Heartburn and offensive smelling eructations. Frequent eructations and discharge of flatulency. Constant nausea, with heat, vertigo and headache. Sickness and nausea, combined with general heat and heat at stomach. Bilious vomiting : after slight errors in diet ; with increased alvine discharges, preceded by tormina. θ Chronic diarrhœa. Feeling of fulness and pressure in epigastrium. θ Dropsy. Feeling of weight in stomach between meals. Heat in stomach before a meal. Chronic gastritis. Sensitive to pressure in right hypochondrium. Pressing pain in region of spleen and left arm. Pinching in region of spleen. Itching under ribs left side. Liver very sensitive to pressure ; ulceration of liver ; ascites from hepatic induration and portal congestion. Skin pale, grayish, dry ; emaciation ; great weakness, can hardly walk across room ; mind impaired ; anorexia ; tongue white and dry ; fulness and pressure in epigastrium ; great tension and dropsical swelling of abdomen ; stools tardy and hard ; scanty, dark-red urine ; painful urination ; scrotum swollen as large as a child's head ; penis S shaped and terribly swollen, prepuce so œdematous as to completely hide orifice of urethra ; dry cough with occasional frothy expectoration ; dyspnœa, can only lie in an elevated position ; anxiety ; sleeps little and but for a short time ; violent, unquenchable thirst ; longs for refreshing drinks ; great œdema of lower limbs, extending from feet to abdomen ; great depression of spirits ; pulse small and frequent ; speech listless ; liver enlarged and indurated, particularly left lobe. θ Affection of liver with dropsy. Sensation of distension from flatulence before stool. Sensation of emptiness in region of navel, with desire to take a deep breath ; > from bandaging or tightening the clothing. Burning, pinching pain in stomach and about navel. Pinching in region of spleen, extending to hip. Pain from left side of chest to groin, < by deep inspiration, particularly in groin and back, like a stitch. Great tension and dropsical swelling of abdomen. Ascites from hepatic disorder. Ascites from enlarged and indurated liver, in consequence of drinking whisky.

stool_rectum.text: Bilious diarrhœa, with aversion to coffee

Soft small stools in the morning after drinking coffee, and again in the evening, with protrusion of the hæmorrhoids. Watery stools in the morning after rising. Frequent passages of flatus and eructations (with constriction of the anus). Stool pappy, yellowish-brown, fetid, with tenesmus and prolapsus ani. Protrusion of the anus during an evacuation. Constipation; stools infrequent and hard. Itching within and around the anus, in the perineum (evening).

Frequent passage of flatus, and belching, with constriction of anus. Watery diarrhœa in morning after rising, protrusion of anus during stool. Offensive, watery, yellowish-brown stools, in the morning after coffee. Soft, small stools in morning after drinking coffee, and again in evening, with protrusion of hemorrhoids. Stools pappy, yellowish-brown, fetid, with tenesmus and prolapsus ani. Very loose, bright yellow stools, with a quantity of mucus, preceded by considerable griping. Bilious diarrhœa < during day, soon after drinking, especially warm drinks. Diarrhœa and bilious vomiting. Before stool : viscid, tasteless saliva in mouth ; burning, pinching pain in stomach and about navel ; sensation of distension from flatulence. During stool : protrusion of hemorrhoids ; prolapsus ani ; pain about navel. After stool : tenesmus ; abdominal pain. Aggravation : in morning ; after coffee ; on alternate days, a later hour each time. Tongue vivid red at tip and margins and coated yellow in centre ; appetite good ; evacuations never less than twice a day of liquid, bilious fecal matter, intermixed with frothy mucus ; no griping and no tenesmus ; some sensibility to external pressure in right hypochondrium ; face pale ; muscles soft and flabby ; bilious vomiting after trivial errors in diet, with aggravation of the diarrhœa, stools on such occasions being preceded by tormina. θ Chronic looseness of bowels and bilious vomiting. Six to eight evacuations in twenty-four hours, consisting of "very loose, bright yellow matter and a quantity of mucus," each relief being preceded by considerable griping ; dejections occur chiefly during night, or early in morning ; during day the mainly occur soon after drinking, especially warm fluids. θ Bilio-mucous diarrhœa. Severe pain in lower part of abdomen, with desire to evacuate ; rumbling pain most acute in left side ; followed by a free discharge of yellow, bilious matter about 4 A. M., recurring daily for a week at same hour. Tardy, infrequent and hard stools. θ Dropsy. Constipation, with great varicosis of hemorrhoidal veins. Copious hemorrhage after constipated stool. Protrusion of anus during defecation. Itching within and around anus, in perineum. Pruritus ani.

male.text: Burning in urethra. Sexual passion and desire increased with erections at night, during sleep. Swollen scrotum

Increased sexual desire (in old men) with violent erections all night. Sensation of fulness in both spermatic cords. Highly excessive enjoyment and pleasure during coition. Seminal discharge tardy but free, and without bad after-feeling.

Satyriasis. Sexual passion increased, with erections at night during sleep. Sexual passion strong, with violent erections all night, and desire to cohabit. Highly excessive enjoyment and pleasure during coition, which was not the case before. Seminal discharge not so quick and early as usual, but free and without any bad after-feeling. Increased sexual desire in old men, with violent erections at night. Pollutions without erection. Sudden loss of sexual power ; impotence. Sensation of fulness in both spermatic cords. Yellow drop from urethra in morning. Gleety discharge at night, staining linen yellow. Fulness of glans penis and lower part of scrotum. Dropsical swelling of penis. Penis curved in the shape of an S, and swollen at least four inches in circumference, the prepuce œdematous to such a degree as to hide the orifice of the urethra. Hydrocele. Oily, pungent smelling sweat on genitals. Gonorrhœa.

urinary.text: Scanty, dark. In dropsy, produces frequent and free discharge, with great relief

Free discharge of light-coloured urine, affording relief. Very frequent discharge of light-coloured urine (thirst increased). Whitish purple-coloured sediment in the urine. Intolerable burning in the urethra during and after urination.

Frequent and free discharge of light-colored urine, with increased thirst. (In sick :) Frequent urging to make water, if he cannot immediately it causes pressing pain on vertex, at other times pressing in temples. Involuntary discharge of urine in night. Urine scanty and dark, painful in passing. θ Dropsy. Urine pungent and fetid. Urine alkaline. Whitish or purple-colored sediment. Burning before and after urination, with aching in bladder. After urination : elastic feeling in urinary organs, with subsequent pleasurable sensation.

female.text: Menses copious, frequent, too long. Ulceration of uterus and os. Copious and excoriating leucorrhœa. Nymphomania
Menstruation too early and too copious; the discharge is thick and coagulated. Acrid leucorrhœa; itching.

Nymphomania. Obstinate cases of ulcerated os uteri ; sharp, darting pains about half an inch in length, like streaks of lightning. Too frequent appearance of menses. Much congestion of sexual organs. Menses too early ; too copious ; blood thick and coagulated. Metrorrhagia with, or in alternation with, difficulty of breathing. Leucorrhœa acrid, excoriating, with itching. Itching on left breast and right side of nose. PREGNANCY. PARTURITION. LACTATION. [24] [Fl. ac.]. During pregnancy has much congestion of blood to head. Itching, redness and swelling of right nipple. Nipples very red, sore and cracked.

respiratory.text: Oppression of chest, difficult breathing, great dyspnœa. Hydrothorax

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Fl. ac.]. Weak, hoarse voice. Dryness in larynx and trachea. Itching in larynx, causing hawking and swallowing. Soreness and increased irritability in larynx, with impeded respiration and wheezing. A sore, raw feeling in trachea. Goitre. Oppression of chest, > when bending backwards. Difficult breathing, afternoon and evening. Wheezing respiration. θ Hydrothorax. Great dyspnœa, has to be propped up in bed. θ Ascites. Short, frequent cough, mostly dry, occasionally some whitish, frothy, mucous expectoration. θ Dropsy. Hawking of phlegm mixed with blood, especially in morning.

extremities.text: Inflammation of joints of fingers. Feeling as of a splinter under nail. Nails crumble. Caries and necrosis, especially of long bones. Coccygodynia. Ulcer over tibia

Pain in right shoulder-joint ; shoulder-joints stiffened by rheumatism. Pain in right shoulder-joint extending towards fingers, as if air was passing down. Rheumatism, especially venereal ; arms and shoulders are very lame and painful. Rheumatic pains in left arm, from shoulder to elbow, with lameness. Slight lameness in right arm ; has some difficulty in writing. Trembling in biceps and triceps of right arm. Numbness and powerlessness of hands. Constant redness of hands, especially palms. Burning in hands. Heat and sweat of palms of hands ; moist palms regain their healthy condition. Acute pricking, as with needles, in fingers. Pain in left index finger, as if bone, now and then during day ; whole finger painful internally, particularly in evening. First and second phalanx of left index finger swollen to four times natural size ; first phalanx particularly swollen so that finger is pear-shaped ; on dorsum of finger an opening occasionally occurs from which an ichorous, purulent discharge takes place. Sharp sticking pain at root of thumb-nail. Whitlow. Panaritium ; also simple onychia, ulceration having set in. Nails grow more rapidly ; crumpled, or longitudinal ridges in them. Sharp sticking pain at root of right thumb nail. Acute stitches in right hip-bone. Lameness in left hip. Coxalgia. Œdematous swelling of legs up to abdomen. θ Ascites. Left leg goes "to sleep" easily. Varicosis of lower limbs. Varicose veins upon legs, tend to ulceration. Pain in right knee-joint. Synovitis of knee-joint ; much pain, followed by dropsy. Top of foot œdematous ; instep. Chronic ulcerations on feet, particularly about joints and on tibia ; bones more or less affected. Burning stitches in soles. Feet hot and burn much. Soreness between toes. Soreness of all the corns. Pain in all the limbs, with weakness and numbness. Limbs go to sleep, although he does not lie on them. Feet and hands excessively moist.

skin.text: Varicose veins. Nævi. Ulcers; red edges and vesicles. Decubitus; worse, warmth. Syphilitic rupia. Itching of cicatrices. Feels as if burning vapor were emitted from pores. Itching especially of the orifices, and in spots, worse warmth. Nails grow rapidly. Periosteal abscess. Profuse, sour, offensive perspiration. Syphilitic tubercles. Dropsy of limbs in old, feeble constitutions. Atony of capillary and venous system. Tissues bloated

Old cicatrices become red around the edges, covered or surrounded by itching vesicles, or they itch violently. Burning pains on small spots of the skin. Itching of the skin (in the month of March). Elevated red blotches. Red, round, elevated blood vesicles, resembling little flesh-warts. Varicose veins on the (l.) leg. Caries and necrosis. Ulcerations, esp. after the abuse of Silica.

Sallow skin. Skin grayish-white. θ Dropsy. Burning pains on small spots of skin. Itching of skin. Sensation as if a burning vapor was emitted from the pores of the whole body. Elevated red blotches. Several small round blood-vesicles, elevated, of a light carmine color, soft and compressible and resembling little flesh warts. Skin tubercles on forehead and face, even when ulcerating ; elevated red blotches on palm of hands ; squamous eruptions on body (psoriasis guttata) ; syphilitic erosions, mucous tubercles ; exostoses and nightly pains in bones. Frequent small boils ; carbuncles. Pustules. Dry cutaneous eruptions. Squamous eruptions on body. Nevus of children (on right temple) ; capillary aneurism. Erosions, mucous tubercles. Varicose veins and ulcers. Ulcers : painful ; < from warmth, > from cold ; with copious discharge ; with red borders and vesicles. Old cicatrices become red around edges, covered or surrounded by itching vesicles or they itch violently. Bedsores ; nevi materni ; pimples. Carious ulcers becoming < from too large or too frequently repeated doses of Silic.

_raw: Compatible: after Arsen. in ascites from gin-drinker's liver after Kali c. in hip disease; after Phos. ac. in diabetes. Compare: Coca (fatigue); Coffea (toothache); Citr. ac. and Sep. (aversion to one's family); Oxal. ac. (diarrhœa from coffee); Rhus t. and Ruta (coccygodynia); Silic. (fistula, onychia, bone diseases, coccygodynia); Brom., Iod., Spongia and Kali c. (goître); Staph. (teeth). Followed well by: Sulph., Nit. ac.
Compatible : after Arsen. in ascites from gindrinker's liver ; after Kali carb. in hip disease ; after Phos. ac. in diabetes ; after Silic. in bone affections

Compare : Coca in increased ability to exercise ; Coffea in toothache ; Citr. ac. and Sepia in aversion to one's family ; Oxal. ac. in diarrhœa, < from coffee ; Rhus tox. and Ruta in coccygodynia ; Silic. in dental fistula, onychia, bone diseases and coccygodynia ; Spongia and Kali carb. in goitre ; Staphis. in sensitive teeth

keynotes.text: color="#800000">FLUORICUM ACIDUM Hydrofluoric Acid Especially adapted to chronic diseases with syphilitic and mercurial history. Glabella region bloated. Acts especially upon lower tissues, and indicated in deep, destructive processes, bedsores, ulcerations, varicose veins, and ulcers. Patient is compelled to move about energetically. Complaints of old age, or the prematurely aged, with weak, distended blood vessels. Hob-nailed liver of alcoholics. Goitre (Dr. Woakes) (Kali fluoride produced bronchocele in dogs). Early decay of teeth. Old cases of nightly fevers, coming on periodically.

Fluoric acid acts on the lower tissues of the body much like Silica, which it follows and precedes well. It is useful after the abuse of Silica. The modalities of the two are different; Fluor. ac. having > from cold applications. It acts on the bones, especially the long bones, causing caries and necrosis, and favours the expulsion of the necrosed part. Fistulæ, rectal, dental, and lachrymal come within its sphere. Old cicatrices become redder and itch. Small red spots here and there, by warmth, > in cool place. Red blotches on body which tend to desquamate. Nails grow rapidly. Teeth are deficient in enamel; black, rough, unsightly-looking. Increased sensitiveness of vision and hearing. According to Guernsey Fluor. ac. affects the right ear; left teeth; left hypochondrium; left side of abdomen; right side and nape of neck; right side of back. T. F. Allen has recorded an experience (N. A. J. H., 1886, p. 288) showing the applicability of Fl. ac. in whitlow. A lady spilled some of the acid on her hand and though treated at once with turpentine a few spots escaped on one hand, and these soon gave her great pain, intensely pulsating. The pulsations involved the tip of the thumb especially, though this had not been touched by the acid. It was not red like the spots touched by the acid but was sore to touch, and when pressed there was a sensation as of a splinter under the nail and in the cellular tissue. This lasted some days. The whole hand was swollen and hot, > in cool, open air. In a workman whose hand was exposed to the fumes of the acid there was intense throbbing pains especially in the thumb; suppuration occurred later and was very slow to heal. According to McLachlan, the left hand is affected rather than the right and the pus tends to point on the dorsum of the finger. > From cold washing distinguishes it from Silic. Fl. ac. is also distinguished from Sil. by the general > from walking in the open air. "A constant, irresistible desire to walk in the open air; it does not fatigue," is characteristics of Fl. ac. Hunger predominates. There is from wine like Zinc.; but also from red wine, which is peculiar to Fluor. ac. Abdominal symptoms > by tightening clothes (Nat. mur.─opp. Lach., Hep.). Peculiar symptoms are: Increased ability to exercise his muscles without fatigue. Is less affected by excessive heat in summer or cold in winter. Sensation as of a cold wind blowing under lids, even in warm room; as of air passing down from shoulder-joint to fingers. Numbness of limbs even when not lain on. Motion . Bending backward, and bending head back >. Cold drinks toothache; washing with cold water >. Warm drink diarrhœa. Symptoms seem to go from below upward. Suited to complaints of old age, and premature old age; weakly constitutions, sallow skin, emaciation.

A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. FLUORICUM ACIDUM. Hydrofluoric Acid .- HF. General Analysis.- Acts especially upon the vegetative system, affecting powerfully mucous surfaces, glandular structures and bones, presenting conditions analogous to those the result of perverted nutrition. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Fluoricum acidum) Uncommon buoyancy of mind ; fears nothing, and is self-satisfied. Disposition to anxious ideas, apprehensive. Head.- (Fluoricum acidum) Severe pressing pain in temples, from within outward. Dullness and pressure in occiput. Falling off of the hair. Caries of the temporal bones. Pain along the sutures. Ears.- (Fluoricum acidum) Intolerable itching in both ears. Eyes.- (Fluoricum acidum) Sensation as if the eyelids were open by force, and a fresh wind were blowing on them. Sensation of sand in the eyes. (Caust., Hep. s., Sulph.) Nose.- (Fluoricum acidum) Fluent coryza. Red, swollen, inflamed nose. Mouth.- (Fluoricum acidum) Sensation of warmth in the teeth. Increased flow of saliva. Mouth and teeth coated with mucus in the morning. Rapid caries of teeth. Tongue deeply and widely fissured in all directions, with a large, deep phagedenic-looking ulcer in the center. Face.- (Fluoricum acidum) Crusta lactea, dry, scaly, itches very much. Tubercles in skin of forehead and face, suppurating ; syphilis infantum. Throat.- (Fluoricum acidum) Throat peculiarly sensitive to cold, slightest exposure resulting in inflammation, with increase of pain and impeded deglutition. Constriction in throat, with difficult deglutition ; in the morning, hawking of phlegm mixed with blood. Stomach.- (Fluoricum acidum) Hunger predominates. Thirst, craves refreshing drinks. Frequent eructations. Nausea, eructations, and lassitude. Bilious vomiting after slight errors in diet, with increased alvine discharges, preceded by tormina. Fullness and pressure in epigastrium. Feeling of weight in stomach between meals. Abdomen.- (Fluoricum acidum) Frequent passages of flatus and belchings, which relieve. Great tension and dropsical swelling of the abdomen. Stool.- (Fluoricum acidum) Constipation ; stools infrequent and hard. (Bry.) Urinary Organs.- (Fluoricum acidum) Free discharge of light-colored urine, affording relief. Intolerable burning in the urethra during and after urination. (Canth.) Male Organs.- (Fluoricum acidum) Increased sexual desire, with violent erections at night. Highly excessive enjoyment and pleasure during coition. Seminal discharge tardy but free, and without bad after feeling. Female Organs.- (Fluoricum acidum) Menses too early ; too copious ; discharge thick and coagulated. Acrid leucorrhoea ; itching. Nipples itching, sore, cracked. Upper Limbs.- (Fluoricum acidum) Pain in right shoulder joint, extending toward fingers, with sensation as if air were passing down. Pain in left index finger ; the whole finger is painful internally. Brittleness of the nails. Panaritium ; also simple onychia. Lower Limbs.- (Fluoricum acidum) Oedematous swelling up to the abdomen. Burning stitches in soles ; feet hot and burn. Soreness between the toes. Varicose veins. Generalities.- (Fluoricum acidum) Increased ability to exercise his muscles without fatigue. Lassitude. Loss of strength. Old cicatrices become red around the edges, and itch violently. Sleep.- (Fluoricum acidum) Drowsy and sleepy in the early evening. Sleeplessness, without inclination to sleep ; a short sleep suffices and refreshes him. Dreams toward morning. Fever.- (Fluoricum acidum) General heat with nausea. Profuse, sour, offensive perspiration ; in the afternoon. Aggravation.- (Fluoricum acidum) In morning ; at rest ; on rising ; when standing ; when sitting. Amelioration.- (Fluoricum acidum) From motion ; while walking. Therapeutic Range.- (Fluoricum acidum) In disorders from perverted nutrition, side by side with Silicea , Calcarea , etc. ; diseases of the osseous tissue, particularly of the long bones ; fistulae ; whitlows ; dyspepsia ; diabetes ; varicosis ; ailments from Mercury or Silicea ; enlarged and indurated liver, after abuse of whiskey. Conditions.- (Fluoricum acidum) Complaints of old age, also premature old age, in consequence of syphilitic mercurial dyscrasia. (Merc.)
